MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT ALLSHIFTS MARKETPLACE, LLC'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE

Appearances via Zoom:
Hoda Katebi of Lichten & Liss-Riordan, PC (617.994.5800, hkatebi@llrlaw.com) for plaintiff and moving party;
David Witkin of Fisher & Phillips LLP (916.210.0400, dwitkin@fisherphillips.com) for defendants and opposing parties.

This matter is not argued. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Nurses and Professional Healthcare's motion to compel further responses is granted in part as specified below.

The court concludes that NPH's meet and confer efforts were adequate. Parties are required to make reasonable efforts to resolve discovery disputes but ultimately the court considers the text of the request and the response in deciding whether a motion has merit.

Special Interrogatory No. 1: NPH has shown good cause for seeking identifying information and contact information for workers; these are witnesses, and the information sought, while personal, does not reveal sensitive facts about them. (Puerto v. Superior Court (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1242.) Allshift shall fully produce responsive documents subject to the parties' protective order.

Special Interrogatory No. 2: NPH shall respond to the interrogatory. Providing identifying information for the corporate decisionmakers does not reveal the content of an attorney-client communication.

Special Interrogatory No. 3: The court cannot make sense of either the interrogatory or Allshifts' response. No further response is ordered.

Special Interrogatory Nos. 6, 8, 9: Allshifts' objections lack merit. It shall respond.

Special Interrogatory No. 7: In light of the court's order as to RFP No. 17 and the broad call of this interrogatory, the court does not order further responses. NPH may propound additional targeted discovery about the revenue and profitability effects of classification decisions.

The further responses ordered herein shall be made within two weeks.

NPH seeks sanctions but did not provide a declaration as required by the Rules of Court. The court does not award sanctions on this motion.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Opposition To Motion For Preliminary Injunction, Exhibit B To The Declaration Of Michelle Alicante, And Declaration Of Wyatt Amaral And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Sep-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS CLIPBOARD HEALTH LLC, AND TWOMAGNETS, LLC's Motion To Seal Portions Of Opposition To Motion For Preliminary Injunction.

The unopposed motion by defendants Clipboard Health LLC and TwoMagnets LLC for an order sealing its opposition papers to plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction is granted. These defendants seek to seal portions of Exhibit B to the Declaration of Michelle Alicante in Support of the Opposition to Plaintiff's Motion for Preliminary Injunction and portions of the Declaration of Wyatt Amaral in Support of the Opposition to Plaintiff's Motion for Preliminary Injunction. The moving defendants have shown facts supporting their claim that the material sought to be sealed contains commercially sensitive information that they take steps to keep secret, and that denying this motion would likely result in competitive harm. The court finds based on facts in the Amaral Declaration submitted in support of this motion that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and is to include the date of filing of the document to sealed, complete transaction number and full title of the document to be sealed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS CLIPBOARD HEALTH LLC, AND TWOMAGNETS, LLC's AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (CLIPBOARD HEALTH LLC, TWOMAGNETS, LLC) motion to strike complaint is denied. A successful UCL plaintiff cannot recover damages. (See Bus. & Prof. Code, sec. 17203; Korea Supply Co. v. Lockheed Martin Corp. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1134, 1148.) Nevertheless, Plaintiff may be able to recover damages on its other claims. The cited prayer is therefore not improper within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure, sec. 436, subdivision (a).

Order submitted.
